expectations.text: Output is primarily written in Bahasa Indonesia
expectations.evidence: The entire output — headings, caption body text, notes, and strategy descriptions — is written in Bahasa Indonesia. Only a handful of English loanwords (e.g., 'glowing', 'bestie', 'checkout') appear, which are standard in Indonesian social media copy.

expectations.text: Contains at least 2 distinct copy variations
expectations.evidence: The output contains 3 distinct variations: Variasi 1 (Emosional + PAS), Variasi 2 (Humor Receh + AIDA), and Variasi 3 (Urgency + SLAP). Each uses a different copywriting framework, tone, and opening hook.

GlowNesia appears in all three variations: 'GlowNesia Serum Vitamin C 15%' in Variasi 1, 'GlowNesia Serum Vitamin C 15% hadir buat jadi bestie baru kulit lo' in Variasi 2, and 'GlowNesia itu serum lokal' in Variasi 3, plus in hashtags (#GlowNesia).

expectations.text: Highlights a specific product benefit related to brightening/mencerahkan (not just listing the vitamin C feature)
expectations.evidence: All three variations frame brightening as a tangible benefit rather than just a feature: Variasi 1 says 'kulit udah keliatan lebih cerah dan glowing' (skin looks brighter and glowing); Variasi 2 says 'bikin wajah cerah dalam 14 hari' (makes face bright in 14 days); Variasi 3 says 'mencerahkan & meratakan warna kulit' (brightens and evens skin tone). The benefit is tied to a 14-day timeframe across all variations.

Each variation ends with an explicit CTA: Variasi 1 — 'Klik link di bio sekarang!'; Variasi 2 — 'Gas langsung sebelum promonya bye-bye / Link di bio!'; Variasi 3 — 'Klik link di bio, checkout sekarang!'

expectations.text: Uses casual/informal register appropriate for Gen Z audience (no 'Anda', uses conversational tone)
expectations.evidence: No instances of 'Anda' anywhere in the output. The copy uses distinctly casual/Gen Z Indonesian: 'lo' and 'gue' (informal pronouns), 'nggak', 'udah', 'aja', 'Gas langsung', 'bestie', 'no drama, no ribet', 'bye-bye', 'POV:', and humor like the mirror joke. The register is consistently informal throughout.

expectations.text: Mentions the price point (89 ribu or 89.000 or 89rb or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Price appears in all three variations: Variasi 1 — 'Cuma 89rb aja'; Variasi 2 — 'Cuma 89RB'; Variasi 3 — 'Harga cuma Rp89.000'.

expectations.text: Includes urgency element tied to the promo (hari ini/sekarang/terbatas or similar)
expectations.evidence: Strong urgency elements in all variations: Variasi 1 — 'HARI INI gratis ongkir!' and 'promo ini cuma hari ini'; Variasi 2 — 'hari ini doang: GRATIS ONGKIR!' and 'sebelum promonya bye-bye'; Variasi 3 — 'HARI INI: Gratis ongkir!' and 'cuma berlaku hari ini. Besok? Udah nggak ada.'

claims.claim: Uses PAS (Problem - Agitation - Solution) framework in Variasi 1
claims.evidence: Variasi 1 opens with a problem (skincare mahal tapi muka kusam), agitates it (habis ratusan ribu, cermin nggak bersahabat), then presents the solution (GlowNesia Serum). Structure matches PAS.
Variasi 2 grabs Attention (mirror POV joke), builds Interest (bukan salah lo), creates Desire (cerah dalam 14 hari, harga 89rb), and closes with Action (Gas langsung, link di bio). Follows AIDA.

claims.claim: Uses SLAP (Stop - Look - Act - Purchase) framework in Variasi 3
claims.evidence: Variasi 3 opens with 'STOP SCROLL' (Stop), presents product details and bullet points (Look), builds urgency about the promo expiring (Act), and directs to checkout (Purchase).

claims.claim: Serum gives results in 14 days as stated in the prompt
claims.evidence: All three variations reference the 14-day claim, matching the prompt's 'mencerahkan wajah dalam 14 hari'.

claims.claim: Gratis ongkir promo is today only
claims.evidence: Matches the prompt's 'promo gratis ongkir hari ini aja'. All variations correctly limit the promo to 'hari ini'.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks caption length or Instagram-appropriateness. A 2000-word essay would pass all assertions but be unusable as an actual Instagram caption. Consider adding an assertion like 'Each caption variation is under 2200 characters (Instagram's limit)' to verify platform fitness.

No assertion checks whether hashtags are included, despite hashtags being a standard and important element of Instagram captions. All three variations include relevant hashtags, but this goes untested.

suggestions.assertion: Highlights a specific product benefit related to brightening/mencerahkan (not just listing the vitamin C feature)
suggestions.reason: This assertion is good — it distinguishes benefit-framing from feature-listing. However, it could be strengthened by also checking that the 14-day timeframe is mentioned, since that specificity is what makes the benefit claim credible and was explicitly provided in the prompt.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the key content requirements well and the casual register check is a good discriminating assertion. Main gap is the lack of any platform-fitness check (caption length, hashtag inclusion) — a well-written blog post about GlowNesia would pass all 8 assertions despite being wrong for Instagram.
